target.title: fix(kimi, moonshot): model picker shows wrong models
target.body: ## Summary - Fix: Model picker did not show Kimi or Moonshot models when selected - Cause: `augmentModelCatalog` hook was missing in provider plugins - Improvement: Picker now shows provider models even without API key ## What Changed / User-visible - Added `augmentModelCatalog` hooks to Kimi and Moonshot plugins - Model picker now shows correct models for: - Kimi: `kimi-code`, `k2p5` - Moonshot: `kimi-k2.5`, `kimi-k2-thinking`, `kimi-k2-thinking-turbo`, `kimi-k2-turbo` - Models visible even before API key is configured - "Keep current" selection behaves correctly ## Scope - Integrations (Kimi, Moonshot) - UI (model picker) ## Evidence ### Kimi (Before vs After) | Before (bug) | After (fixed) | |--------------|---------------| | | | - Before: shows models from all providers - After: correctly filtered to Kimi models --- ### Moonshot (Before vs After) | Before (bug) | After (fixed) | |--------------|---------------| | | | - Before: shows Amazon Bedrock models - After: correctly filtered to Moonshot models ## Repro & Verification 1. Run OpenClaw setup wizard 2. Select Kimi or Moonshot provider 3. Open model picker - Verified manually: Before/After images show correct model lists and "Keep current" works - Edge cases checked: Models appear in catalog with correct provider IDs - Runtime API calls with actual API keys not verified ## Risk - Static catalogs may become outdated (same pattern as other providers; dynamic catalogs still used when API key is configured)

commands.author: coygeek

target.title: feat(autoreview): model env defaults and Claude fallback-model
target.body:
## Summary
- add environment defaults for model, thinking, and Claude fallback chains
- forward Claude `--fallback-model` from autoreview
- expand `skills/autoreview/SKILL.md` with a models/thinking matrix, Claude aliases, and env-var table

## Why
Model selection is already wired via `--model` / `--thinking`, but discoverability and zero-flag defaults were missing. Claude Code docs also expose `--fallback-model` for overload/retired models ([model-config](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config)).

## Environment precedence
CLI flag > per-engine CLI (`engine=value`) > global CLI > per-engine env > global env.

Supported env vars:
- `AUTOREVIEW_MODEL`, `AUTOREVIEW_THINKING`, `AUTOREVIEW_FALLBACK_MODEL`
- `AUTOREVIEW_<ENGINE>_MODEL`, `AUTOREVIEW_<ENGINE>_THINKING`, `AUTOREVIEW_CLAUDE_FALLBACK_MODEL`

## Validation (doc-backed / dry-run; no live Claude subscription)
```bash
$ ruby scripts/validate-skills
validated 5 skills

$ AUTOREVIEW_MODEL=gpt-5.4 AUTOREVIEW_CLAUDE_FALLBACK_MODEL=sonnet,haiku \
  python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --engine claude --dry-run
engine: claude
model: gpt-5.4
fallback_model: sonnet,haiku

$ python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --engine claude --fallback-model haiku --dry-run
fallback_model: haiku

$ python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --engine codex --fallback-model x --dry-run
--fallback-model is only supported for claude, not codex
```

## Notes
- Supersedes/extends the docs-only scope of #17 with runtime env + fallback support.
- Droid thinking docs remain model-only until #16 lands.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: coygeek
